description: CDC Pipeline terminology glossary — shared across all agents and skills for consistent language

graph.definition: A directed graph with typed nodes and weighted edges, representing either code structure or documentation structure

node.definition: An entity in the graph (e.g., function, class, service, concept, endpoint)
node.schema: See graph-schema.md for 29 valid node types

edge.definition: A directed relationship between two nodes (e.g., calls, implements, documents)
edge.schema: See graph-schema.md for 44 valid edge types

batch.definition: A subset of files grouped for parallel LLM processing; each batch becomes one worker task

worker.definition: An LLM subagent that processes a single batch (code-batch-analyzer) or document (doc-freetext-analyzer)

coordinator.definition: An agent that orchestrates deterministic phases (scripts) and delegates LLM phases to workers

orchestrator.definition: The top-level skill/workflow that coordinates the entire pipeline

side.definition: Which graph we're talking about: 'code' (source code) or 'doc' (design documents)

alignment.definition: The process of matching nodes across code and doc graphs to find correspondences

consistency.definition: The degree to which code and documentation agree on entities, relationships, attributes, and behavior

diff.definition: A structured difference between code and doc graphs, classified by type and severity

drift.definition: A difference where both sides have the same entity but attributes have diverged over time

coverage.definition: What percentage of code entities have corresponding doc entries (and vice versa)

summary.definition: A 1-3 sentence natural language description of a node's purpose and behavior
summary.qualityRule: Must be >10 chars, not equal to name or file path

confidence.definition: A label indicating how reliable a node/edge/alignment is (high/medium/low)

symbol_index.definition: A global index of all exported symbols across code files, used for cross-file resolution

workspace.definition: The _workspace directory under the project root where all intermediate pipeline artifacts are stored

artifact.definition: Any intermediate or final JSON file produced by a pipeline phase

phaseNames.BATCH: Semantic file grouping for parallel processing

languageNotes.english: Use English for all script variable names, JSON keys, and code. Use Chinese in agent prompt prose for readability.
languageNotes.chinese: Agent prompts use Chinese prose (中文说明) but English technical terms (e.g., 'batch', 'worker', 'coordinator') are kept in English for precision.
